todaysEvents.summary: Trump tells reporters US will be 'leaving Iran in 2-3 weeks'; White House announces prime-time address 9pm ET; Rubio says US can see the 'finish line' - Brent fell -13.2% to $100.64 confirmed (Reuters, NPR, DW Apr 1).

Iran fires 7 missiles at Israel with cluster munitions; 16 injured including children; one strike near US Embassy branch Tel Aviv; Houthis claim 'third operation' against Israel in coordination with Iran and Hezbollah; first Houthi cluster munition use confirmed (Jerusalem Post, Magen David Adom Apr 1).

IRGC issues ultimatum threatening 18 US tech firms - Apple, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, Intel, Cisco, HP, Oracle, IBM, Dell, Palantir, JP Morgan, Tesla, GE, Boeing, Spire Solutions, G42 - deadline 8pm Tehran time; Iran drones Kuwait Airport fuel tanks igniting large fire; Kuwait Aviation Fueling facility damaged (CNBC, KUNA, Jerusalem Post Apr 1).
todaysEvents.delta: IRGC tech ultimatum; Kuwait airport hit

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump's 'leaving in 2-3 weeks' declaration catalyses a back-channel breakthrough: toll booth suspended, Iran accepts partial sovereignty language, Trump refrains from Kharg strikes and resumed power plant operations, Houthis stand down Yemen-Israel campaign. Prime-time address April 1 announces ceasefire framework. Brent retreats to $85-95/bbl; JKM pulls back to $15-18/MMBtu on resumed Qatar damage assessment. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Trump's peace rhetoric fails to translate into ceasefire: Iran continues missile strikes and Kuwait infrastructure attacks while demanding Hormuz sovereignty. IRGC tech company threat causes selective cyberattacks on Western firms. Tehran Toll Booth ($2M/vessel in yuan) new normal; Kharg invasion talk pressures Iran but no seizure executed. Houthis sustain third-front operations. April 6 deadline extended again. Brent settles $95-120/bbl; JKM at $17-22/MMBtu. iTraxx Asia IG holds 175-185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Prime-time address announces Kharg Island seizure or resumed power plant strikes; Iran revokes non-hostile framework and closes toll corridor; full commercial blockade reinstated. IRGC executes cyberattacks on US tech firms - Apple, Nvidia supply chains disrupted. Houthis escalate to daily Israel strikes; ground invasion preparations confirmed. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter. Oil hits $150-180/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges.
